title: Orthodon chinensis
taxonomy: Orthodon chinensis is classified as a species of freshwater fish in the family Cyprinidae. This family is commonly known as the carp or minnow family. The genus Orthodon is further classified under the subfamily Labeoninae, which comprises small to medium-sized cyprinids. The exact classification of Orthodon chinensis may vary slightly depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the family Cyprinidae. This classification is based on the fish's physical characteristics and morphological features.

intro: Orthodon chinensis, also known as Chinese Orthodon, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to China and other parts of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Orthodon chinensis include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often made into tea, capsules, or tinctures, and is also used in combination with other herbs to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
